pg49-p1-s1: around each other at incredible speed.
pg49-p1-s2:
These tiny bodies are governed by precise laws, and these laws hold true
throughout the material world.
pg49-p1-s4: We have no reason to doubt it.
pg49-p1-s5:
When, however, the perfectly logical
assumption is suggested that underneath the material world and life as we see it, there is an All Powerful, Guiding,
Creative Intelligence, right there our perverse streak comes to the surface and we laboriously set out to convince
ourselves it isn't so.
pg49-p1-s6:
We read wordy books and indulge in windy arguments, thinking we believe this universe needs no
God to explain it.
pg49-p1-s7:
Were our contentions true, it would follow that life originated out of nothing, means nothing, and
proceeds nowhere.
pg49-p1:
around each other at incredible speed. These tiny bodies are governed by precise laws, and these laws hold true
throughout the material world. Science tells us so. We have no reason to doubt it. When, however, the perfectly logical
assumption is suggested that underneath the material world and life as we see it, there is an All Powerful, Guiding,
Creative Intelligence, right there our perverse streak comes to the surface and we laboriously set out to convince
ourselves it isn't so. We read wordy books and indulge in windy arguments, thinking we believe this universe needs no
God to explain it. Were our contentions true, it would follow that life originated out of nothing, means nothing, and
proceeds nowhere.
pg49-p2-s1:
Instead of regarding ourselves as intelligent agents, spearheads of God's ever advancing Creation, we agnostics and
atheists chose to believe that our human intelligence was the last word, the alpha and the omega, the beginning and end
of all.
pg49-p2-s2: Rather vain of us, wasn't it?
pg49-p2:
Instead of regarding ourselves as intelligent agents, spearheads of God's ever advancing Creation, we agnostics and
atheists chose to believe that our human intelligence was the last word, the alpha and the omega, the beginning and end
of all. Rather vain of us, wasn't it?
pg49-p3-s1: We, who have traveled this dubious path, beg you to lay aside prejudice, even against organized religion.
pg49-p3-s2:
We have
learned that whatever the human frailties of various faiths may be, those faiths have given purpose and direction to
millions.
pg49-p3-s3: People of faith have a logical idea of what life is all about.
pg49-p3-s4:
Actually, we used to have no reasonable
conception whatever.
pg49-p3-s5:
We used to amuse ourselves by cynically dissecting spiritual beliefs and practices when we might
have observed that many spiritually-minded persons of all races, colors, and creeds were demonstrating a degree of
stability, happiness and usefulness which we should have sought ourselves.
pg49-p3:
We, who have traveled this dubious path, beg you to lay aside prejudice, even against organized religion. We have
learned that whatever the human frailties of various faiths may be, those faiths have given purpose and direction to
millions. People of faith have a logical idea of what life is all about. Actually, we used to have no reasonable
conception whatever. We used to amuse ourselves by cynically dissecting spiritual beliefs and practices when we might
have observed that many spiritually-minded persons of all races, colors, and creeds were demonstrating a degree of
stability, happiness and usefulness which we should have sought ourselves.
